title: Context Matters for Meaning
concept: To understand a word, you need to remember preceding words (e.g. 'not' changes the meaning of everything that follows).

exercises.prompt: To understand the true meaning of a word in-the-moment words, what does an AI need to remember:

options.text: The preceding words that came before it

exercises.explain: Preceding words (the words that come before) give context and help us understand the true meaning of the words that follow.

cards.title: The Mystery Word
cards.body: If you hear someone say "bark", are they talking about a dog or a tree? Without the rest of the sentence, even the smartest computer can't tell!

cards.title: What is Context?
cards.body: Context is the set of words that come before and after a specific word. It acts like a helper that tells us what a word actually means in a sentence.

cards.title: The Detective Novel
cards.body: Imagine reading a mystery novel. If you only look at the very last page, you won't understand who the villain is. You need to remember the clues from the chapters before it to make sense of the ending!

cards.title: The 'Not' Flip
cards.body: Consider the sentence: "I wanted to go, but I did not." If an AI forgets the word "not", it will think you actually went! The word "not" completely flips the meaning of "go".

cards.title: Do Computers Just Read Lists?
cards.body: Many people think AI reads sentences word-by-word like a simple list. In reality, modern AI looks at how words connect to each other. It must keep track of previous words to truly understand the current one.

cards.title: Context in Action
cards.body: See how the meaning changes completely when we include the words that came before!

exercises.prompt: Why is the word 'not' so important for an AI to remember in a sentence?

options.text: It changes the meaning of everything that follows it.
It speeds up how fast the computer reads.
It does not change the meaning at all.

exercises.explain: The word 'not' reverses the action that follows it. Without keeping track of it, the AI gets the opposite meaning.

exercises.prompt: True or False: A computer can easily understand the word 'bank' (river bank vs. money bank) without looking at any other words in the sentence.
exercises.explain: AI must look at the surrounding words (context) to know which meaning of a word is correct.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to complete the explanation of word relationships:
exercises.sentence: The word 'not' changes the meaning of 'happy', which is why AI needs context to understand sentences.
exercises.explain: The word 'not' changes the meaning of 'happy', showing why computers need the surrounding context to understand sentences.

exercises.prompt: An AI is writing a story about someone having a terrible day. Which sentence uses context correctly?

options.text: My day was good because I lost my keys and it rained.
My day was not good because I lost my keys and it rained.
My day was happy because I lost my keys and it rained.

exercises.explain: The first sentence has a typo and says 'good' instead of 'not good'. The second sentence uses 'not' to correctly change the meaning of 'good' to describe a terrible day.

exercises.prompt: When training an AI to understand human language, what does 'context' mean?

options.text: The font style of the words
The surrounding words that give a word its meaning
The speed at which the computer reads text
The number of letters in a sentence

exercises.explain: In AI language processing, 'context' refers to the surrounding words that give a specific word its true meaning. Without context, words like 'it' or 'not' lose their power.
